platformInstall.description: Platform-specific install steps. Keys are platform IDs (macos, linux, windows). The installer picks the entry matching the current platform; falls back to the top-level install field if no match. Registry Completion (v3.0): every package must declare all three keys explicitly - a real installStep where the software exists on that platform, or { unsupported: true, reason } where it genuinely doesn't. A platform key must never be silently omitted.

binary.description: The executable name to probe for on PATH, when it differs from (or isn't reliably inferable from) versionCommand/validate's first word and the package name itself - see core/environment/discovery.js's binaryNameFor(). Optional: most packages don't need this because the inference already works.

versionCommand.description: Shell command whose output contains this component's installed version (Compatibility Engine - core/compatibility/versions.js). Optional: falls back to parsing `validate`'s output, then to 'unknown' - never guessed beyond that.

recommendedAlternatives.description: Names of other registry packages that serve a similar purpose and may be worth considering (Compatibility Engine repair suggestions).

environment.description: Environment Configuration Engine metadata (core/environment/) - PATH entries, variables, and raw shell lines this package needs, generated into a single owned shell file rather than requiring manual .zshrc edits. Only path/variables/shell are implemented so far; aliases/functions/completions/startupCommands/shutdownCommands/validation/repair/uninstall are planned but not yet processed by the engine - see docs/EnvironmentEngine.md.

path.description: Directories to add to PATH, as shell expressions (may reference $HOME/$VARNAME - expanded at shell-startup time, not at generation time).

variables.description: Environment variables this package needs. Each value is either a literal shell expression (`value`, may reference $HOME/other vars) or a shell command whose stdout becomes the value at shell-startup time (`command` - e.g. resolving a keg-only Homebrew formula's real install path), never both.

shell.description: Raw shell lines to append verbatim (e.g. `eval "$(mise activate zsh)"`). Trusted, registry-authored content - embedded as-is, not escaped.

unsupportedPlatform.description: An explicit, intentional declaration that this software genuinely does not exist on this platform (e.g. Xcode on Linux) - Registry Completion (v3.0) requires this instead of silently omitting the platform key, so 'nobody has checked yet' and 'this doesn't exist here' are never confused.

platformInstallEntry.description: Either a single installStep, a { unsupported, reason } declaration, or (Linux/Windows only, where more than one mainstream package manager is genuinely viable - apt/dnf/pacman, winget/choco/scoop) an array of installSteps, one per method. installer.js's resolveInstallStep() picks the array entry whose method matches the actually-detected package manager on the running machine, so a single package manifest correctly serves an apt user AND a dnf user AND a pacman user instead of assuming one distro family.

platformInstall.description: Platform-specific install steps for this variant. Same Registry Completion (v3.0) rule as the top-level platformInstall: every key explicit, real installStep or unsupportedPlatform.
